title: Zambian Authorities Corpus Record
description: A single citation-grade record of a Zambian primary legal authority (Act, Statutory Instrument, judgment, constitution, regulation, guideline, directive, or gazette notice). Every record MUST be reproducible from its source_url + source_hash.

id.description: Stable, human-readable slug. Lowercase, hyphen-separated. Must be unique across the corpus.

type.description: Authority type. Drives downstream rendering and citation format.

jurisdiction.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Always 'ZM' for this corpus.

title.description: Official short title as published. Do not paraphrase.

citation.description: Canonical citation string as it would appear in a legal pleading (e.g. 'Act No. 10 of 2017', 'SI No. 84 of 2018', '[2021] ZMSC 12').

date_of_assent.description: Date the instrument was assented to (Presidential signature) or, for non-Acts, the date it was issued/decided. ISO 8601 calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD). For Zambian Acts, this is the date stamped 'Date of Assent' on the Government Printer copy. Distinct from commencement (which is the date the SI appointing operation is published) and from enactment (which is the date Parliament passed the Bill).

commencement_date.description: Date the instrument came into force. May differ from enacted_date. ISO 8601 calendar date.

in_force.description: True if currently in force as of fetched_at. False if repealed or expired. Null if unknown.

version_type.description: For acts, sis, constitution, and regulations: whether this record captures the as-enacted text, a later consolidation, a standalone amendment, or is uncharacterised. Null permitted for records where the distinction does not apply (e.g. judgments, gazette notices) or is genuinely unknown.

consolidated_as_of.description: For records with version_type = consolidated: the date up to which amendments are incorporated (i.e. the consolidation cut-off). ISO 8601 calendar date.

amended_by.description: IDs of records that amend this one. Each ID must resolve to another record in the corpus (integrity check).

repealed_by.description: ID of the record that repealed this one, if any. Must resolve to another record in the corpus.

sections.description: Structured sections, used for Acts, SIs, regulations, the constitution, and any instrument that is divided by section/article number.

number.description: Section number as published, including any letter suffix (e.g. '1', '12A', '12(2)(b)').

title.description: Section title / marginal heading as published. v0.3 rename of v0.2 'heading'. Verbatim from source — typos preserved.

part.description: Roman numeral of the Part this section belongs to (e.g. 'I', 'XVII'), or null if the instrument is not divided into Parts.

part_title.description: Title of the containing Part, verbatim from source (e.g. 'PRELIMINARY PROVISONS' — sic). Source typos preserved.

page_start.description: 1-indexed PDF page number where this section begins in the source PDF. Null if the section was not located on a body page (skeleton entries that failed cross-reference).

body.description: Verbatim section body text. v0.3 rename of v0.2 'text', now nullable. Skeleton parses (Phase 2 pilot) carry null bodies and populate them in Phase 4 bulk parse. Golden sections and Phase-4-parsed sections carry the full text. No paraphrasing. No interpolation.

subsections.description: Optional nested subsections, same shape as the parent.

paragraphs.description: Structured paragraphs, used for judgments. Numbered as published in the judgment.

court.description: For judgments only. Court that issued the decision.

case_number.description: For judgments only. Court file / case number as published.

parties.description: For judgments only. Named parties to the action.

judges.description: For judgments only. Names of judges on the panel, in the order printed.

cited_authorities.description: For judgments only. IDs of other corpus records (statutes, judgments) that this judgment cites. Each ID must resolve.

source_url.description: Canonical URL the raw bytes were fetched from. Must be the URL on the authoritative host, not a mirror or cache.

source_hash.description: sha256 of the raw fetched bytes, prefixed with 'sha256:'. Used for re-verification (Phase 7).

fetched_at.description: Time the raw bytes were fetched. ISO 8601 UTC with 'Z' suffix. No fractional seconds.

parser_version.description: Semantic version of the parser that produced this record. Bump on any parsing-logic change.

notes.description: Free-form provenance and editorial notes. Used for: source typo annotations preserved verbatim; pilot-record qualifications; commencement-SI deferrals; bound-volume vs PDF page count discrepancies; observed-but-not-source-asserted amendment chains. Not validated for structure — but everything written here MUST be source-attestable in the same way as the rest of the record (no fabrication, no interpolation).

then.description: Judgments must include court and case_number.
Statutory instruments must carry a sections array (may be empty only for stub records explicitly flagged in gaps.md).
Consolidated records must declare the date up to which amendments are incorporated.
